validationNotes.rationale: This is a code review/analysis mission - the deliverable is a comprehensive code review report (REVIEW_REPORT.md) documenting findings across 96 files. The synthesis milestone finalizes the report that synthesizes all module and cross-cut reviews. Validation is completeness-based (report exists with proper organization, severity ratings, cross-cut patterns), not behavior-based through user interaction.

VAL-REPORT-001.evidence: REVIEW_REPORT.md exists at repository root with 592 lines, organized by severity (Critical, High, Medium, Low, Info) and category sections

VAL-REPORT-002.evidence: All 162 findings include file paths (e.g., 'crates/liteclip-core/src/encode/ffmpeg/mod.rs'), location references (function names, line ranges), issue descriptions, impact analysis, and suggested fixes

VAL-REPORT-003.evidence: Severity definitions documented in report header: Critical (crashes, data loss, security vulnerabilities), High (significant bugs, major architectural issues), Medium (quality issues, minor bugs), Low (style, minor improvements), Info (observations, potential future work). All 162 findings appropriately categorized with 0 Critical, 17 High, 57 Medium, 45 Low, 43 Info.

VAL-REPORT-004.evidence: Cross-Cutting Patterns section identifies 8 systemic patterns: 5 negative patterns (FFmpeg unsafe blocks lacking SAFETY docs, Thread join without timeout, Error swallowing, Mutex contention, Bytes.clone zero-copy) and 3 positive patterns (Hardware encoder fallback, DXGI access lost recovery, Pipeline fail-closed health enforcement)

userReportedIssues.originalReport: some excess cpu and memory usage, some related to the ui
rootCausesIdentified: Audio preload memory (full track decoded into memory)
Spin loop with 1ms sleep on full frame channel
Multiple Mutex acquisitions per playback frame
Frame pool allocates ~24-40MB per video editor
Thread spawning per video (decoder, audio stream, audio preload)
